When I speak to business groups about how the scarcity principle drives the success of limited number and limited time offers, I regularly get a version of the following question. But what if we don't have a limited supply or limited time frame for what we offer? What if we can deliver as much as the market demands? How can we use the power of scarcity? The solution is to recognize that scarcity doesn't only apply to the count or availability of items, but as well to the specific features of the items. Identify a factor in your product or service that is unique or so uncommon that it can't be obtained elsewhere at the same price or at all. As studies show, that consumers are strongly attracted to products and experiences that possess a unique element. Then mark it honestly on the basis of that unique feature and the benefits that will be lost if it is missed. Now if the item doesn't have a single such feature, it may well possess a unique combination of features that can't be matched by your competitors. In that case, the scarcity of that unique set, that unique suite of features can be marketed honestly and quite effectively.
